title: MVP Loop Design Brief — Padel AI Platform
voice: third-person professional; capability-conditional; no first-person pronouns

stages.input: One full-match smartphone video plus minimal metadata (court, opponents, self-declared level)
stages.action: Operator runs Joao-M-Silva open-source pipeline as a tracking scaffold; human corrects rally boundaries and shot labels against a fixed taxonomy
stages.output: Per-rally event log: rally start/end, shots per player, shot type, court zone, outcome
success_metric: ≥90% of rallies correctly delimited on a held-out 50-rally control set
Shot-label agreement ≥0.7 Cohen's kappa vs domain coach on 200-shot sample
stages.failure_mode: Tagging cost per match exceeds EUR 25 — unit economics break for any plausible price tier

feasibility_anchor.verified_quote: Position and velocity of each player
feasibility_anchor.secondary_quote: Position and velocity of the ball
feasibility_anchor.hardware_floor_verified: 8 GB VRAM at default batch size

stages.input: Structured event log plus prior matches if any
stages.action: Cluster losing-shot patterns (per VM-003); compute Elo-style rating delta; select two drills from coach-curated drill library
stages.output: One-page recap: top 3 losing-shot clusters, rating delta, two drills with situation, success criterion, partner role
success_metric: Recap generated within 24 hours of upload
stages.failure_mode: Insight too generic to be actionable — drill-acknowledgement <10% kills the template

calibration_basis.verified_quote: ≥10% of uploads convert to drill plan acknowledgement within seven days
calibration_basis.interpretation: MVP target lifted to 40% inside one club due to coach reinforcement and social proof

stages.input: Coach session schedule plus the recap from Stage 2
stages.action: Coach reviews recap, picks one of the two drills, runs it; files one-tap outcome rating (improved/unchanged/worse) post-session
stages.output: Drill-to-outcome record: drill, losing-shot cluster, improvement signal — seeds the supervised dataset
success_metric: Coach files outcome tag for ≥70% of recaps
Within four weeks, ≥30% of players show a measurable shift in their dominant losing-shot cluster
stages.failure_mode: Coach refuses tool the student also touches — canonical SEG-003 caveat

moat_role.verified_quote: Each match generates unique-per-user data that improves the rating model
moat_role.interpretation: Stage 3 outcome tag is the only step that converts a video stream into a labelled supervised-learning signal

core_loop.strongest_mechanism: Stage 3 outcome tag — converts video into labelled supervised-learning signal; canonical Naval data gate per VM-001

manual_first_mvp.scope: one academy in one club, no product code

hardware.secondary_fallback: club camera (Eyes On Padel / Wingfield / Clutch) only if phone path blocked

pipeline_steps: Player or coach uploads match video to a shared cloud folder
Operator drops video into Joao-M-Silva pipeline as tracking scaffold
Operator and part-time padel-literate tagger correct rally boundaries and label shots in spreadsheet
Operator runs Elo-style rating-delta calculation in spreadsheet column
Operator selects two drills from coach-curated drill library
Operator drafts templated PDF/email recap; sends from candidate's address (not branded domain)

wizard_of_oz_anchors.author: Jared Friedman, Y Combinator
wizard_of_oz_anchors.verified_quote: Build a prototype of your product, but have a human do the things you planned to do with fancy algorithms

wizard_of_oz_anchors.author: Jared Friedman, Y Combinator
wizard_of_oz_anchors.verified_quote: If it is, you can always build the algorithms to replace the humans

wizard_of_oz_anchors.author: Sara Paul and Maria Rosala, Nielsen Norman Group
wizard_of_oz_anchors.verified_quote: A moderated research method in which a user interacts with an interface that appears to be autonomous but is (fully or partially) controlled by a human

wizard_of_oz_anchors.author: Sara Paul and Maria Rosala, Nielsen Norman Group
wizard_of_oz_anchors.verified_quote: works best for testing interfaces powered by complex technologies — such as conversational UIs, learning algorithms, and real-time information systems

wizard_of_oz_anchors.author: Marty Cagan / SVPG (body of work)
wizard_of_oz_anchors.note: SVPG canonical concierge teaching present across Cagan body of work; primary verbatim not extracted in this pass

category_corroboration.verified_quote: Hudl Assist uses a combination of professional human analysts and, in some sports, advanced AI technology to tag every key moment
category_corroboration.interpretation: Category leader still relies on human analysts for the long tail of sports — padel is the long tail

category_corroboration.verified_quote: computer vision to automatically track player movements, ball contacts, and other key actions
category_corroboration.interpretation: Volleyball precedent shows the manual-to-automated transition pattern within one vendor

promotion_criteria_manual_to_automated.manual_phase: Human corrects rally boundaries and shot labels after Joao-M-Silva first pass
promotion_trigger: Cumulative human correction rate ≤15% on rally boundaries across 200 consecutive matches
Cumulative human correction rate ≤20% on shot labels across same window
promotion_criteria_manual_to_automated.automated_successor: Thresholded confidence rejection — only low-confidence rallies route to a human

anchor.verified_quote: Rating delta correlates with self-assessed level r ≥ 0.55 across 50 matches

promotion_criteria_manual_to_automated.manual_phase: Operator drafts each recap from a Markdown template; hand-picks two drills
promotion_trigger: Coach panel NPS on recap content ≥+30
promotion_criteria_manual_to_automated.automated_successor: Templated recap generated by an LLM with the drill library as a retrieval index; operator reviews only outliers

promotion_criteria_manual_to_automated.manual_phase: Coach files outcome tags via WhatsApp or single-page form; operator transcribes
promotion_trigger: ≥70% recaps closed with an outcome tag
≥10 confirmed drill-to-outcome transitions per losing-shot cluster
promotion_criteria_manual_to_automated.automated_successor: Outcome tagging integrated into coach-side mobile flow; drill-to-outcome model promoted from rule-based to learned

academy_size_anchor.secondary_quote: nearly 300 groups per year for 3- to 4-day camps

red_team_anchor.verified_quote: recruit 8 coaches via FEP and FITP chapters and run a 1-week pilot

iteration_discipline: shot taxonomy: max one open issue per week
drill library: max one swap per week
recap template: max one block change per week

kill_criteria.trigger: Operator-plus-tagger time per match exceeds 75 minutes after week 3
Recap-open <30% sustained 2 weeks OR drill-acknowledgement <10% over same window
Two or more coaches in the panel decline to file outcome tags in a given week despite reminders

anchor.verified_quote: Coaches sometimes refuse any tool the student also touches

line_items.assumption: Candidate is the founder; not billed
European sport-analyst freelance lower band; specific Padelytics rate not verified
8 GB VRAM commodity tier; specific provider quote not captured

hardware_anchor.verified_quote: 8GB of VRAM using default batch sizes

line_items.assumption: Coach hourly rate mid-band; Spanish padel pricing reference verified

price_band_anchor.note: Reference page lists Spanish padel coaching prices; specific EUR 60 mid-point is candidate's calibration

veto_rights_scope: any phrasing in recap touching coaching content

anchor.verified_quote: Run JTBD interviews with 8 plateau-stuck regulars and ask explicitly whether 'rating that travels' or 'drill prescription' was the precipitating switch

structural_vetoes: Shot taxonomy lock — no taxonomy change ships without sign-off from a coach with ≥10 hours of tagging time
Recap content review — no recap goes to a player without a coach having read at least one recap from that week's batch

failure_modes_ranked.name: Recap sent but never read (engagement death)
failure_modes_ranked.detection: Recap-open rate <30% over 2 weeks; tracked via tracking pixel or unique read URL per recap
mitigation: Tighten trigger language to partner-comparison framing per SEG-001 FM-3
Move recap delivery from email to WhatsApp where local read rates dominate
Couple recap to a coach 1:1 — coach is the delivery channel

failure_modes_ranked.name: Coach refuses tool the student also touches
failure_modes_ranked.detection: Coach outcome-tag rate <50% sustained 2 weeks despite reminders; anonymous coach exit-interview at week 3
mitigation: Coach-only mode by default — player never sees recap unless coach forwards it
Recap framed as the coach's artefact, not the platform's

anchor.verified_quote: Coaches sometimes refuse any tool the student also touches

failure_modes_ranked.name: Insight too generic to be actionable (commodity content)
failure_modes_ranked.detection: Drill-acknowledgement rate <20% sustained; coach NPS on recap content <0
mitigation: Drill library curated by domain coaches, not the candidate

failure_modes_ranked.name: Camera/phone occlusion in indoor clubs
mitigation: Fixed-camera-position protocol PDF (behind one baseline, 2.5 m, 30° tilt)
Fallback tag mode unknown_outcome that human resolves rather than discards
Club-camera vendor fallback (Eyes On Padel) only if needed by week 4

hardware_anchor.verified_quote: 8GB of VRAM using default batch sizes

failure_modes_ranked.name: Rating drift across clubs (federation effect failure)
failure_modes_ranked.detection: Same player's rating drifts >5% across two clubs in a 14-day window (forward-flag, out of single-club scope)
mitigation: Rating spec published as a fixed schema per VM-001 next_90_days
Cross-club expansion gated on VM-002 LOIs (two regional FIP-affiliated organisers)

anchor.verified_quote: publish the shot taxonomy as the schema VM-011 will adopt

verified_sources.topic: Open-source padel CV pipeline; feasibility anchor
Y Combinator on Wizard of Oz approach (Jared Friedman)
Nielsen Norman Group definition of Wizard of Oz method
Marty Cagan / SVPG body of work on concierge tests
Hudl Assist FAQ — manual analysts plus AI
M3 Padel Academy size reference (25 coaches, ~300 groups/year)
Spanish padel coaching prices reference band
Eyes On Padel club-camera vendor fallback

data_gaps: Specific Padelytics, Hudl-Iberia, or peer freelance tagger hourly rate not verified against a primary source
Specific cloud GPU EUR/hr quote for an 8 GB VRAM tier not verified
Specific Marty Cagan SVPG verbatim quote on concierge-vs-Wizard distinction not extracted; only INFERRED from body of work
Average academy size (number of coaches per padel academy in Spain/Italy) not verified beyond one anchor (M3 Marbella)
Padelytics public pricing tier not disclosed on landing
decision_for_interviewer: Is the operating model expected to compound the B2B2C coach co-pilot (CC-2) before or after the B2C plateau-stuck-regular loop is monetised? Coach-first locks switching-cost moat (VM-004) earlier; player-first compounds the data moat (VM-001) earlier. Both cannot be the lead motion.
strongest_mechanism_summary: Stage 3 outcome tag — converts video into labelled supervised-learning signal (drill → outcome → rating shift); canonical Naval data gate per VM-001
most_probable_failure: Recap engagement death (mode 1) — fast detection, structural mitigation by moving delivery channel from candidate-owned email to coach-owned conversation

claudeMd_rules_checked: MUST NOT fabricate quotes — every quote tagged VERIFIED has source_url and verbatim ≤15-25 words
MUST NOT first-person — voice is third-person professional throughout
MUST NOT promise budgets or headcount — phrasing is capability-conditional; coach contract explicitly excludes equity
MUST tag every claim VERIFIED / INFERRED / ABSENT — applied
MUST run numeric calculation through sandbox — cost envelope is explicit arithmetic, not LLM-generated
